articles.title: n8n Webhooks Abused Since October 2025 to Deliver Malware via Phishing Emails
articles.summary: Threat actors have been observed weaponizing n8n, a popular artificial intelligence (AI) workflow automation platform, to facilitate sophisticated phishing campaigns and deliver malicious payloads or fingerprint devices by sending automated emails. articles.title: Sweden Blames Pro-Russian Group for Cyberattack Last Year on Its Energy Infrastructure
articles.summary: In what was Sweden’s first public mention of the attack, the country’s minister for civil defense said it targeted a heating plant in western Sweden. The post Sweden Blames Pro-Russian Group for Cyberattack Last Year on Its Energy Infrastructure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: UK warns businesses to address cyber risks amid Anthropic AI panic
articles.summary: The British government warned businesses to strengthen their cyber defenses amid concerns prompted by the release of Anthropic's Mythos over how artificial intelligence could reshape the threat landscape.

articles.title: CISA flags Windows Task Host vulnerability as exploited in attacks
articles.summary: CISA warned U.S. government agencies to secure their systems against a Windows Task Host privilege escalation vulnerability that could allow attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges. [...]

articles.title: Exploited Vulnerability Exposes Nginx Servers to Hacking
articles.summary: Hackers are exploiting CVE-2026-33032, a critical remote takeover vulnerability in the Nginx UI management tool. The post Exploited Vulnerability Exposes Nginx Servers to Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Educational company McGraw Hill says Salesforce misconfiguration led to data leak
articles.summary: The data breach emerged this weekend when the ShinyHunters cybercriminal organization claimed to have stolen 45 million Salesforce records and threatened to leak the information by April 14 if a ransom was not paid.

articles.title: Rolling Networks: Securing the Transportation Sector
articles.summary: Modern trucks are rolling networks packed with sensors, connectivity, and attack surfaces, creating new cyber risks. NMFTA's Cybersecurity Conference brings industry leaders together to tackle emerging threats in transportation. [...]

articles.title: ‘By Design’ Flaw in MCP Could Enable Widespread AI Supply Chain Attacks
articles.summary: Researchers warn that a flaw in Anthropic’s Model Context Protocol allows unsanitized commands to execute silently, enabling full system compromise across widely used AI environments. The post ‘By Design’ Flaw in MCP Could Enable Widespread AI Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: 100 Chrome Extensions Steal User Data, Create Backdoor
articles.summary: Published through five accounts, the extensions appear part of a coordinated campaign based on shared C&C infrastructure. The post 100 Chrome Extensions Steal User Data, Create Backdoor appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISO Conversations: Ross McKerchar, CISO at Sophos
articles.summary: Sophos’ Ross McKerchar discusses leadership at scale, retaining talent, defending against AI-enabled threats, and the industry’s growing trust problem. The post CISO Conversations: Ross McKerchar, CISO at Sophos appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Actively Exploited nginx-ui Flaw (CVE-2026-33032) Enables Full Nginx Server Takeover
articles.summary: A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting nginx-ui, an open-source, web-based Nginx management tool, has come under active exploitation in the wild. articles.summary: A number of critical vulnerabilities impacting products from Adobe, Fortinet, Microsoft, and SAP have taken center stage in April's Patch Tuesday releases. articles.title: Sweden says pro-Russian hackers attempted to breach thermal power plant
articles.summary: A suspected pro-Russian hacker group attempted to disrupt operations at a thermal power plant in western Sweden last year, a Swedish defense official said.

articles.title: Two Vulnerabilities Patched in Ivanti Neurons for ITSM
articles.summary: The flaws could allow a remote attacker to maintain access after their account has been disabled and to access information from other user sessions. The post Two Vulnerabilities Patched in Ivanti Neurons for ITSM appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: $10 Domain Could Have Handed Hackers 25k Endpoints, Including in OT and Gov Networks
articles.summary: Researchers found adware capable of killing cybersecurity products and pushing more dangerous payloads to infected systems. The post $10 Domain Could Have Handed Hackers 25k Endpoints, Including in OT and Gov Networks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Fortinet Patches Critical FortiSandbox Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: The flaws could allow attackers to bypass authentication or execute arbitrary code or commands via HTTP requests. The post Fortinet Patches Critical FortiSandbox Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft Issues Patches for SharePoint Zero-Day and 168 Other New Vulnerabilities

articles.title: Microsoft adds Windows protections for malicious Remote Desktop files
articles.summary: Microsoft has introduced new Windows protections to defend against phishing attacks that abuse Remote Desktop connection (.rdp) files, adding warnings and disabling risky shared resources by default. [...]

articles.title: Crypto-exchange Kraken extorted by hackers after insider breach
articles.summary: The Kraken cryptocurrency exchange announced that a cybercrime group is trying to extort the company by threatening to release videos showing internal systems that host client data. [...]

articles.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
articles.summary: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." articles.title: New ‘JanaWare’ ransomware targeting Turkish citizens as cybercriminal ecosystem fragments
articles.summary: The researchers said the ransomware operation has been ongoing since 2020 and is associated with a strain of malware that enforces execution constraints based on system locale and external IP geolocation.

articles.title: Microsoft Patches Exploited SharePoint Zero-Day and 160 Other Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Experts say this is the second-largest Microsoft Patch Tuesday ever based on CVE count. The post Microsoft Patches Exploited SharePoint Zero-Day and 160 Other Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: McGraw-Hill confirms data breach following extortion threat
articles.summary: Education company McGraw-Hill has confirmed in a statement to BleepingComputer that hackers exploited a Salesforce misconfiguration and accessed its internal data. [...]

articles.title: Adobe Patches 55 Vulnerabilities Across 11 Products
articles.summary: Critical ColdFusion vulnerabilities are the most at risk of being exploited in attacks, according to the software giant. The post Adobe Patches 55 Vulnerabilities Across 11 Products appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: New PHP Composer Flaws Enable Arbitrary Command Execution — Patches Released

articles.title: AI-Driven Pushpaganda Scam Exploits Google Discover to Spread Scareware and Ad Fraud

articles.title: 5 Ways Zero Trust Maximizes Identity Security
articles.summary: Stolen credentials remain a top breach vector, often leading to unchecked privilege escalation. Specops explains how identity-first Zero Trust limits access, enforces device trust, and blocks lateral movement. [...]

articles.title: ‘Mythos-Ready’ Security: CSA Urges CISOs to Prepare for Accelerated AI Threats
articles.summary: CISOs face a shrinking window to prepare as AI models like Mythos collapse the gap between vulnerability discovery and exploitation, driving a new era of high-velocity cyberattacks. articles.title: Nightclub Giant RCI Hospitality Reports Data Breach
articles.summary: The company said in an SEC filing that an IDOR vulnerability affecting RCI Internet Services exposed contractor data. The post Nightclub Giant RCI Hospitality Reports Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Organizations Warned of Exploited Windows, Adobe Acrobat Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: The security defects allow attackers to escalate privileges and execute arbitrary code remotely. The post Organizations Warned of Exploited Windows, Adobe Acrobat Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: European Gym giant Basic-Fit data breach affects 1 million members
articles.summary: Dutch fitness giant Basic-Fit announced that hackers breached its systems and gained access to information belonging to a million of its customers. [...]

articles.title: Stolen Rockstar Games analytics data leaked by extortion gang
articles.summary: Rockstar Games has suffered a data breach linked to a recent security incident at Anodot, with the ShinyHunters extortion gang now leaking the stolen data on its data leak site. [...]

articles.title: Critical flaw in wolfSSL library enables forged certificate use
articles.summary: A critical vulnerability in the wolfSSL SSL/TLS library can weaken security via improper verification of the hash algorithm or its size when checking Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA) signatures. [...]

articles.title: OpenAI rotates macOS certs after Axios attack hit code-signing workflow
articles.summary: OpenAI is rotating potentially exposed macOS code-signing certificates after a GitHub Actions workflow executed a malicious Axios package during a recent supply chain attack. [...]

articles.title: New Booking.com data breach forces reservation PIN resets
articles.summary: Booking.com has confirmed via a statement to BleepingComputer that it has detected unauthorized access to its systems that has exposed sensitive reservation and user data. [...]

articles.summary: Banks and financial institutions in Latin American countries like Brazil and Mexico have continued to be the target of a malware family called JanelaRAT. articles.summary: The cybersecurity industry is obsessing over Anthropic’s new model, Claude Mythos Preview, and its effects on cybersecurity. articles.title: FBI, Indonesia take down W3LL phishing tool
articles.summary: A widely used phishing tool that allowed hackers to create fake websites that looked like legitimate login portals for just $500 was disrupted by the FBI and law enforcement agencies in Indonesia.

articles.title: Adobe rolls out emergency fix for Acrobat, Reader zero-day flaw
articles.summary: Adobe has released an emergency security update for Acrobat Reader to fix a vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-34621, that has been exploited in zero-day attacks since at least December. [...]

articles.summary: The online travel platform has not said how many customers’ booking information was exposed, but said the issue has been contained. The post Booking.com Says Hackers Accessed User Information appeared first on SecurityWeek.
